tasks.title: Redirect GlobalLockDir in start_test.go
tasks.body:
Three test functions in cmd/daemon/start_test.go invoke the start command without redirecting GlobalLockDir, causing them to acquire the real ~/.wolfcastle/daemon.lock:

- TestStartCmd_ValidationErrors (line ~18)
- TestStartCmd_ValidationWarnings (line ~41)
- TestStartCmd_VerboseFlag (line ~120)

Add the standard redirection pattern to each function, immediately after creating the test environment:

    lockDir := t.TempDir()
    dmn.GlobalLockDir = lockDir
    defer func() { dmn.GlobalLockDir = "" }()

The established pattern is visible in start_validation_test.go for reference. Ensure the dmn import (internal/daemon) is present.

tasks.title: Redirect GlobalLockDir in start_coverage_test.go
tasks.body:
TestStartCmd_AlreadyRunning_OwnPID (line ~17) in cmd/daemon/start_coverage_test.go invokes the start command without redirecting GlobalLockDir.

Add the standard redirection pattern:

    lockDir := t.TempDir()
    dmn.GlobalLockDir = lockDir
    defer func() { dmn.GlobalLockDir = "" }()

Ensure the dmn import (internal/daemon) is present.

Add the standard redirection pattern:

    lockDir := t.TempDir()

Ensure the dmn import (internal/daemon) is present.

tasks.title: Run affected tests and verify no real locks created
tasks.description: Run affected tests and verify no real locks created
tasks.body:
Run all tests in cmd/daemon/ and verify:

1. All tests pass: go test ./cmd/daemon/ -run 'TestStartCmd_ValidationErrors|TestStartCmd_ValidationWarnings|TestStartCmd_VerboseFlag|TestStartCmd_AlreadyRunning_OwnPID|TestStartCmd_StalePIDRecoveredBeforeStart' -v
2. No daemon.lock file is created in ~/.wolfcastle/ during the test run (check before and after)
3. The full cmd/daemon test suite still passes: go test ./cmd/daemon/ -count=1

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Fix Leaking Tests is complete and correct

breadcrumbs.text: Added GlobalLockDir redirection to three test functions in cmd/daemon/start_test.go that were leaking the real daemon.lock
Added GlobalLockDir redirection to three test functions in cmd/daemon/start_coverage_test.go that were leaking the real daemon.lock
Added GlobalLockDir redirection to TestStartCmd_AlreadyRunning_ErrorContainsPID in cmd/daemon/start_deep_coverage_test.go
Verified all affected tests pass and daemon.lock is untouched: 5/5 named tests green, full suite green, lock mtime unchanged

Audit reviewed 3 files (start_test.go, start_coverage_test.go, start_deep_coverage_test.go) totaling ~240 lines of test code across 7 modified test functions. PASS. All functions correctly apply the lockDir := t.TempDir() / dmn.GlobalLockDir = lockDir / defer reset pattern matching the convention in start_validation_test.go. The dmn import was correctly added where missing. Build, tests (with -race), formatting, and vet all clean. One escalation: TestStartCmd_AlreadyRunning in daemon_test.go:672 is a pre-existing test that also leaks the real daemon.lock but was not in this node's scope.

audit.result_summary: Fixed GlobalLockDir leakage in 7 test functions across cmd/daemon/start_test.go, start_coverage_test.go, and start_deep_coverage_test.go. Each function now redirects dmn.GlobalLockDir to t.TempDir() with a deferred reset, matching the convention already established in start_validation_test.go. Verification confirmed all tests pass with the race detector, the real daemon.lock file is untouched during test runs, and no formatting or vet issues exist. One pre-existing leak in daemon_test.go:TestStartCmd_AlreadyRunning was escalated.

audit.objective: Verify all GlobalLockDir redirection fixes are complete and correct
audit.what_happened: Reviewed all 3 modified files (7 test functions). Confirmed the lockDir pattern is consistent across all functions, imports are correct, build/test/format/vet all clean. Identified one pre-existing leak in daemon_test.go:TestStartCmd_AlreadyRunning and escalated it.
went_well: Clean mechanical changes, zero issues introduced by this node
Escalation caught a pre-existing leak that would otherwise go unnoticed
action_items: Fix TestStartCmd_AlreadyRunning in daemon_test.go:672 with the same GlobalLockDir redirect pattern

task-0001.objective: Redirect GlobalLockDir in start_test.go so tests stop acquiring the real daemon.lock
task-0001.what_happened: Added the standard lockDir := t.TempDir() / dmn.GlobalLockDir = lockDir / defer reset pattern to TestStartCmd_ValidationErrors, TestStartCmd_ValidationWarnings, and TestStartCmd_VerboseFlag. The dmn import was already present. All three tests pass, full cmd/daemon suite green.
went_well: Straightforward mechanical fix, pattern already well-established in start_validation_test.go
improvements: None; the fix is minimal and matches the existing convention exactly

task-0002.objective: Redirect GlobalLockDir in start_coverage_test.go so tests stop acquiring the real daemon.lock
task-0002.what_happened: Added the standard lockDir := t.TempDir() / dmn.GlobalLockDir = lockDir / defer reset pattern to TestStartCmd_AlreadyRunning_OwnPID, TestStartCmd_ValidationBlocksWithErrors, and TestStartCmd_StalePIDRecoveredBeforeStart. Added the dmn import that was missing from this file. All three tests pass, full cmd/daemon suite green.
went_well: Straightforward mechanical fix matching the established convention
improvements: None; the fix is minimal and matches existing convention exactly

task-0003.objective: Redirect GlobalLockDir in start_deep_coverage_test.go to prevent leaking the real daemon.lock
task-0003.what_happened: Added the standard lockDir redirection pattern to TestStartCmd_AlreadyRunning_ErrorContainsPID, the only test in this file that executes the start command. Added the missing dmn import. The other tests in the file (TestRecoverStaleDaemonState_* and TestStartCmd_FlagsRegistered) operate on temp dirs or only inspect flags, so they don't need redirection. Note: the task description referenced TestStartCmd_StalePIDRecoveredBeforeStart at line ~53, but that function lives in start_coverage_test.go and was already fixed by task-0002.
went_well: Quick identification that only one of the six tests in the file actually needed the fix

task-0004.objective: Verify all affected tests pass and no real daemon.lock is created during test runs
task-0004.what_happened: Ran all five named tests with -v (all passed). Captured the modification timestamp of ~/.wolfcastle/daemon.lock before and after the full cmd/daemon suite; timestamp was unchanged (1774057785), confirming no test touched the real lock. Full suite passed clean in 6s.
went_well: All fixes from tasks 0001-0003 hold under both targeted and full-suite runs
Lock file modification timestamp comparison cleanly proves no leakage
improvements: None; this was a pure verification task
